title: Release Notes & Frequently Asked Questions | Respiratory Illnesses | CDC
text:

December 23, 2025 release 
1. Season Outlook Update on Data and Trends Page
What : 
In collaboration with the Center for Forecasting and Analytics (CFA), the Season Outlook was updated to confirm that respiratory disease season is expected to be similar to the patterns observed in the 2024-2025 season. 
Why: 
CDC updates the Season Outlook every two months during the respiratory season to keep estimates for COVID-19, RSV, and flu as accurate as possible based on current conditions. 
December 19, 2025 release 
Revision of Activity Levels Data Notes 
Data Notes for “Level of Respiratory Illness Activity” have been updated to explain how we calculate illness activity based on emergency department visits shown in the related graphic. Additional revisions were made to specify the steps taken to calculate these values in special cases, such as hospital service areas (HSAs).
2. Revision of Other Illness Going Around Page and Accompanying Data Notes
What: 
Respiratory condition definitions were revised based on feedback from CDC clinicians and subject matter experts. Some codes were excluded, or the grouping was adjusted. See data notes for detailed, updated definitions. 
“Respiratory Conditions Treated in the Emergency Department” was renamed to “Respiratory Conditions Reporte
